name: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal
title: Commander of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment

ina_flag.title: INA Flag
ina_flag.description: The flag of the Indian National Army symbolized the courage, sacrifice, and determination of those who fought for India's freedom under Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose.

political_document.title: Political Document
political_document.description: Represents Captain Lakshmi Sahgal's commitment to India's independence, leadership in the Indian National Army, and her vision of a free and united nation.

timeline.title: Early Life
timeline.story: Born as Lakshmi Swaminathan in Madras, she studied medicine and developed a deep commitment to serving society.

timeline.title: Doctor in Singapore
timeline.story: While practicing medicine in Singapore, she provided care to the Indian community and came into contact with the Indian independence movement.

timeline.title: Joining the Indian National Army
timeline.story: Inspired by Netaji Subhas Chandra Bose, she joined the Indian National Army to contribute directly to India's struggle for independence.

timeline.title: Rani of Jhansi Regiment
timeline.story: She became the commander of the Rani of Jhansi Regiment, one of the world's first all-women combat units, inspiring women to take an active role in the freedom movement.

timeline.title: Leadership and Service
timeline.story: She led and trained women soldiers with discipline and determination while continuing to serve as a physician whenever needed.

timeline.title: Legacy
timeline.story: Captain Lakshmi Sahgal is remembered as a fearless freedom fighter, compassionate doctor, and an enduring symbol of women's leadership in India's independence movement.
